//: Renovate configuration for the Anchor monorepo.
Single most important setting: minimumReleaseAge. We refuse to even
OPEN a PR for a dependency upgrade until the upstream release is at
least N days old. This is the cooldown that catches supply-chain
attacks like the npm shai-hulud worm, the Ultralytics PyPI compromise,
and the various LangChain/MCP typosquats — those bad versions are
almost always yanked within 48-72h, so a 7-day floor means we
Per-category overrides below tighten or loosen this depending on
blast radius: runtime deps (7d), dev/test tools (3d, less risky),
GitHub Actions (14d, CI compromise is high-impact), majors (30d,
Renovate is run as a GitHub App (https://github.com/apps/renovate).
Enable it on the repo via the GitHub UI; no token needed in the repo.

vulnerabilityAlerts.//: Security fixes bypass the cooldown — if there's a known CVE we want the patch ASAP. Renovate still verifies the fix version isn't itself the compromised one (it checks the advisory).

packageRules.//: Dev / test tools: shorter cooldown. Pytest/ruff/typescript-eslint don't run on user machines, so a compromise here is bounded to CI + our laptops. 3 days is still enough to catch the typical bad-publish-then-yank cycle.

GitHub Actions: longer cooldown. A compromised Action runs with our CI's permissions, can write to checked-out trees, and (in release.yml) is the thing PyPI's OIDC trusts. 14 days is conservative on purpose.
Major version bumps: always 30 days minimum + manual review. Majors often carry breaking changes that take weeks for the community to triage; we don't want to be on the bleeding edge.
Patch + minor bumps for dev deps: auto-merge after CI passes. Saves human attention for what matters. Runtime deps stay manual.
Group React + ReactFlow + adjacent ecosystem so their bumps land together rather than 5 sequential PRs.
Group Vite + its plugins. Same reasoning.
Group Tailwind + PostCSS + the styling toolchain.
Group the Radix UI primitives — they release in coordinated waves.
Group the FastAPI / Starlette / uvicorn family — same release coordination.
Pydantic + pydantic-settings + the OpenAI SDK that depends on it — keep them in lockstep.
PyMuPDF + Docling: the PDF ingest stack. Tightly coupled bbox semantics — bumping one without the other has bitten us. Force them into one PR.
Pin Python to 3.12 unless we deliberately decide to bump. Renovate's default is to chase the latest, which would risk breaking the wheel's classifiers.

python.//: uv's lockfile is renovate-compatible since renovate v37. No extra config needed.
